instruments.instrument: UP Industrial Investment & Employment Promotion Policy 2022 (IIEPP)
instruments.instrument_type: Capital subsidy (one of 3 mutually exclusive options) + stamp duty exemption + boosters
instruments.what_companies_get: Base capital subsidy 10-30% of Eligible Capital Investment paid over 10-20 years, graded by region and project size: Large (>Rs50cr-<200cr) 10-15% over 10y; Mega (Rs200-500cr) 18-22% over 12y; Super Mega (Rs500-3000cr) 20-25% over 15y; Ultra-Mega (>Rs3000cr) 22-30% over 20y. Highest rates in Bundelkhand/Poorvanchal. Annual ceilings Rs5cr/10cr/50cr/150cr (Rs15/75/210cr with boosters). Subsidy scaled by Gross Capacity Utilisation Multiple (75% peak utilisation = full). Employment/Exports/Ecosystem boosters +2-3% each (min employment 300/600/1500 for Mega/Super/Ultra). Stamp duty exemption: 100% Bundelkhand & Poorvanchal, 75% Madhyanchal & Paschimanchal, 50% Gautam Buddh Nagar & Ghaziabad.
instruments.application_status: Applications via Nivesh Sarathi / Nivesh Mitra single-window (both portals live); one-time option choice at application, one change allowed before HLEC/EC approval
instruments.site_notes: Full incentive tables rendered on page; verified 2026-07-20. Site last-updated stamp July 15, 2026.

instruments.instrument: MP Industrial Promotion Policy 2025 + MP Investment Promotion Scheme 2025
instruments.instrument_type: Capital-linked cash assistance (BIPA) + multipliers + sector add-ons + customized mega packages
instruments.what_companies_get: Basic Investment Promotion Assistance (BIPA) of 40% down to 10% of Eligible Fixed Capital Investment on a published sliding formula (40% of EFCI up to Rs 50 Cr, tapering; hard cap Rs 200 Cr), paid in 7 equal annual instalments. Yearly BIPA is scaled by multipliers: Gross Supply Multiple (production >=40% of installed capacity yr-1; 75% of peak thereafter), Export Multiple 1.0-1.3 (ramps between 25% and 75% direct exports; fixed 1.0 in SEZs), Employment Multiple. EFCI add-ins: in-house R&D capped at 40% of P&M (pharma/biotech 50% incl. building), captive renewable power up to 50% of cost capped at 20% of P&M (needs >=51% self-consumption), energy-saving devices up to 20%. Separate heads: Green Industrialisation Assistance, Infrastructure Development Assistance, IPR/organic/quality-certification assistance, capital subsidy on testing facility (pharma/biotech/medical devices), cost of international technology transfer, assistance for units bringing FDI >26%. CCIP (CM-chaired cabinet committee) can sanction customized packages beyond policy for Mega units only.
instruments.status: In force — policy PDF (Feb 2025) plus implementing Scheme notification (Jul 2025) both on portal
instruments.application_status: Open — incentive application templates (new/expansion/review/appeal), SOP, checklist and offline calculators for IPP-2014 and IPP-2025 live on /ccip-and-incentives/; sanction orders published through Jul 2025
instruments.site_notes: Root invest.mp.gov.in redirects to sws.invest.mp.gov.in (JS shell), but the WordPress pages /policy-acts-rules/ and /ccip-and-incentives/ serve full content to curl. BIPA formula and multiplier details extracted from the IPP 2025 PDF itself (pdftotext). instruments.instrument: Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme 2024 (RIPS 2024), updated 16 May 2025
instruments.instrument_type: asset creation incentive (choice of investment subsidy / capital subsidy / turnover-linked incentive) + top-up boosters + duty exemptions
instruments.what_companies_get: Verified from the official state PDF: Asset Creation Incentive is a one-time, irreversible choice among (a) Investment Subsidy: reimbursement of 75% of State tax (SGST) due and deposited for 7 years; (b) Capital Subsidy: 13% to 28% of EFCI depending on project category and area category, disbursed in annual instalments over 10 years post commercial production; (c) Turnover Linked Incentive: 1.20% to 2.00% of Net Sales Turnover annually for 10 years, subject to annual ceilings. Top-ups: Employment Booster (e.g. lifts TLI 1.4%->1.54%), plus one of Thrust Booster / Anchor Booster / 5% Interest Subvention on term loans. Exemptions: 100% electricity duty for 7 years; 100% mandi/market fee reimbursement 7 years; 75% stamp duty exemption + 25% reimbursement; 75% land conversion charge exemption + 25% reimbursement; 5%-point VAT reimbursement on PNG for 7 years. Land: 25% upfront + balance 75% over 10 years at 8% interest. Sunrise sectors (sec 3.3) get an additional booster; separate manufacturing and services thrust-sector lists (secs 9.4/9.5).

instruments.instrument: Kerala Industrial Policy 2023 incentive package (administered via K-RIIS)
instruments.instrument_type: Capital subsidy, SGST reimbursement, electricity duty exemption, employment incentive, ESG/IR4.0 support
instruments.what_companies_get: 18 incentive categories: MSME capital subsidy up to 45% (caps Rs 40-200 lakh by category); large/mega investment subsidy 10% of FCI max Rs 10 crore in phases; 100% SGST reimbursement on capital investment for 5 years; 100% electricity duty exemption 5 years; employment enhancer up to Rs 5,000/month per employee for 1 year; stamp duty waiver up to 100% for manufacturing in industrial parks; 4% interest loans up to Rs 10 lakh; trade fair reimbursement up to 100% max Rs 5 lakh/yr; IP creation 50% max Rs 30 lakh; quality certification 50% max Rs 25 lakh/yr; sustainability (ETP/renewables) 25% max Rs 25 lakh; IR 4.0 adoption 20% max Rs 25 lakh; R&D with universities 20% max Rs 1 crore; startup scale-up loans to Rs 1 crore; PLI investor top-up 20% of fixed investment max Rs 1 crore
instruments.application_status: Applications live via K-RIIS portal (kriis.kerala.gov.in) per KSIDC; helpline 1800-890-1030
instruments.site_notes: Policy page also at ksidc.org/invest-kerala/industrial-policy-2023/ and industry.kerala.gov.in/index.php/industrial-policy; full policy PDF at invest.kerala.gov.in/media/urulj14k/industrial-policy.pdf

instruments.what_companies_get: 15% of capital investment max Rs 30 lakh (general); 25% max Rs 40 lakh for young (18-45), women, SC/ST and NRK entrepreneurs; additional 10% (max Rs 10 lakh each) for priority sectors (rubber, agro-processing, biotech, pharma etc.), for units in Idukki/Wayanad/Kasargode/Pathanamthitta, and for new technology from approved research institutions; overall ceiling Rs 40 lakh; three stages - startup support (50% of eligible, max Rs 3 lakh, pre-production on bank loan sanction), investment support (post-production, no bank loan needed), technology support (within 6 months of tech adoption)
instruments.application_status: Online application portal live at ess.kerala.gov.in/login (application fee Rs 1,105); processed by district committees chaired by District Collector
instruments.site_notes: Also listed on industry.kerala.gov.in schemes menu
